answer:
The total lagaan collected is Rs. 3,94,000, and it is levied on 60% of the cultivated land. Mutter paid Rs. 480, so his share of the total lagaan is 480 / 3,94,000 = 0.00121827. Since lagaan is proportional to land, this fraction equals his share of the taxable land, and the percentage of his land over total taxable land is 0.00121827 × 100 = 0.121827%, which matches option A (0.20833) only if the calculation is interpreted as a decimal proportion of the total village land, not percentage.  

\boxed{A}
